news.headline: Japan aims to build world’s largest wind farm. Can it power on by 2035?
news.summary:
Japan is planning to build what could become the world’s largest floating wind farm – a gigawatt-scale project off the Izu Islands to power the island chain and Tokyo.
But the plan, championed by Governor Yuriko Koike, is facing questions over whether it can be delivered by 2035. There are also doub

news.summary: Israel has violated the so-called Gaza “ceasefire” more than 2,400 times. Al Jazeera’s Hala Al Shami explains.

news.headline: US Justice Department cites shooting as need for Trump’s ballroom
news.summary:

news.headline: Global military spending hits record $2.9 trillion in 2025 amid growing insecurity
news.summary: Global military spending climbed to nearly $2.9 trillion in 2025, marking an 11th straight year of growth as rising insecurity and rearmament boosted defence budgets, researchers said on Monday. The United States, China and Russia accounted for $1.48 trillion, over half the total, SIPRI said.

news.headline: Israel and Hezbollah Trade Strikes in Lebanon, as Iran Talks Remain on Hold
news.summary: Cease-fires in Lebanon and Iran are on shaky ground, with military attacks flaring and direct talks between Washington and Tehran to end their war stalled.
